sections.title: Overview
sections.content: Simple wound closure may be considered for selected clean low-risk lacerations when this is within scope and clinically useful, but many wounds are better irrigated, dressed, and referred. Steri-Strips are often the most appropriate low-complexity option for superficial low-tension wounds.

sections.title: Steri-Strip Application
sections.content:
1. Ensure the wound is clean, haemostasis is achieved, and the skin edges are dry

2. Approximate the edges gently without blanching or excessive tension

3. Apply strips across the wound from the centre outward to maintain alignment

4. Reinforce with a suitable dressing and clear follow-up advice

sections.title: Simple Interrupted Suturing
sections.content:
1. Only close wounds that are clean, suitable, and within local scope and competence

2. Irrigate thoroughly, provide appropriate analgesia or local anaesthesia, and prepare the field aseptically

3. Approximate the skin edges with simple interrupted technique or the approved closure method for that wound and location

4. Reassess wound alignment, bleeding control, and distal function afterward

5. Dress the wound and provide clear aftercare and review advice

sections.title: Key Points
sections.content:
- Do not close contaminated wounds, bites, devitalised wounds, or wounds needing formal surgical exploration
- Irrigation and assessment matter more than rushing to close a wound
- When in doubt, dress and refer rather than performing a marginal closure
- Document the closure method, materials, wound location, and follow-up advice
